scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify that mapRow correctly handles empty precursor annotations and charge 'NA' defaulting to '0'.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Check that when precursor annotations are empty, pr_transitions is set to an empty list, and when charge is 'NA', the key uses '0' as charge state.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must process the row even if precursor annotations are empty, and default charge 'NA' to '0'.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If the function returns early due to empty precursor annotations (wrongly) or does not default charge, mappings will be incorrect.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(row, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Introduce test cases where fragment or precursor annotations are empty or missing to verify graceful handling.
Add test cases with different charge state values: NA, empty, integer, and verify that keys are built correctly.
